steps.expected: Go and Python have identical xmlns:mbp handling
steps.observed: 394/394 — all toc.ncx files match. Both Go and Python omit xmlns:mbp when no mbp: elements are present.

steps.action: Check SecretsCrown toc.ncx for xmlns:mbp in both Go and Python
steps.observed: Neither Go nor Python has xmlns:mbp declaration. Neither uses mbp: prefix. MATCHING behavior.

tocNCX() generates mbp:meta and mbp:meta-img elements when Description/Icon present (L322-330). xmlns:mbp declaration omitted when no mbp: elements used — matches Python's cleanup_namespaces behavior.

PASS (all 5 sub-tests):
- mbp_namespace_declaration: Confirms xmlns:mbp absent when no mbp: elements
- mbp_meta_description: Confirms mbp:meta description generated
- mbp_meta_img_masthead: Confirms mbp:meta-img generated
- periodical_ncx_classes: Confirms section/article classes
- no_periodical_classes_when_not_magazine: Confirms no class on regular books

summary: Tested 4 assertions: all 4 passed. NCX hierarchy, page list, NAV landmarks, and xmlns:mbp namespace all match between Go and Python for all 10 test books. Original 6 books are at 394/394 (0 diffs). HeatedRivalry and SunriseReaping have matching navigation files. 1984 has one minor page anchor fragment difference (#page_134) which is an upstream content resolution issue, not a navigation code issue. SecretsCrown navigation differences are solely due to filename convention (VAL-EPUB-002), not navigation logic. All Go unit tests for NCX and nav generation pass.
